ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, INTERVENORS ON BEHALF OF THEIR SUSPENDED INSURED DEFENDANT HAMILTON SUPPLY COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. MR. ROCHA'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND DECLARATION, HAMILTON'S 1975 CERTIFICATE OF OWNERSHIP (INTERVENORS' EXHIBIT O) AND PLAINTIFFS' EXHIBITS C AND D CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MR. ROCHA WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. SPECIFICALLY, THEY CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER HAMILTON SUPPLY COMPANY, INC. IS LIABLE FOR OAKLAND SHEET METAL SUPPLY CO., INC. DURING THE TIME PERIOD AT ISSUE HERE. ORDER SIGNED, BUT IT WILL BE FILED UPON THE COURT'S RULINGS ON DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS. MS. OZOLS IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER ON DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: ERIN ROBINSON, CSR 12199 OF AIKEN WELCH (503/TLJ)

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT AVOCET ENTERPRISES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND THE ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUES 1, 2 AND 3 (NEGLIGENCE, STRICT LIABILITY AND LOSS OF CONSORTIUM) AND TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS TO ISSUE 4 (PUNITIVE DAMAGES). MR. ROCHA'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND DECLARATION, DEFENDANT'S RESPONSES TO GENERAL ORDER 129 INTERROGATORIES AND MR. MACARTHUR'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MR. ROCHA WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS (VENTFABRICS FLEXIBLE DUCT CONNECTORS) ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFFS REPRESENT THAT THEY HAVE DISMISSED THEIR CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED AND WILL BE FILED WHEN THE COURT COMPLETES THE RULINGS ON DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: ERIN ROBINSON, CSR 12199 OF AIKEN WELCH (TEL: 510.451.1580) (503/TLJ)

calendar.matters:
Specially Appearing United States Fire Insurance Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation Of California; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-30-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters are heard concurrently:
CGC-09-275294 Jodie Collins VS. Asbestos Defendants (B/P)
CGC-10-275498 Betty Peterson VS. Asbestos Defendants (B*P)
CGC-12-276026 Eugene Rocha, et al VS. Asbestos Corporation Limited, et al

APPEARANCES:
Richard M. Grant of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff
K. Albert Loquaci and Ethan Seibert of Nicolaides Fink Thorpe...LLP (415-890-5252, appearing for Specially Appearing United States Fire Insurance Company

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Specially-Appearing United States Fire Insurance Company's Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation of California is denied. U.S. Fire lacks standing to bring this motion or appear in the action at this time. (W. Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal. App. 4th 1196, 1205 n. 12 ["Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 387, the trial court has discretion to permit a nonparty to intervene where the nonparty has a direct and immediate interest in the action"].) U.S. Fire has not demonstrated that it remains liable for any default judgment against Associated. (Id.)

Court takes Judicial Notice of Specially Appearing US Fire Insurance Company's Exhibits 1-6.
Order signed in open court.

Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: S. Le. Court Reporter: Katy Schmidt CSR #13096 (Tel: 916-673-7338 email: kschmidtcsr@gmail.com). (503/CML)

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Grant Wilson, Incorporated
Off Calendar Dec-12-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

Chris Herson, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, EUGENE ROCHA AND GAIL ROCHA.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) GRANT WILSON, INCORPORATED.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED, AND DECREED that the Plaintiff EUGENE ROCHA and Plaintiff GAIL ROCHA shall recover damages from defendant GRANT WILSON, INCORPORATED in the amount of $578,900.00 in Economic Damages, $3,000,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, for a total amount of $3,578,900.00. The Judgment shall take effect immediately. The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT AVOCET ENTERPRISES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances:
Ryan Kujawski of Howard Rome Martin & Ridley LLP (650.365.7715 x226) for defendant and moving party; Jennifer Alesio and Briana McCarthy of Brayton Purcell (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: summary judgment is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2 and 3 (negligence, strict liability and loss of consortium) and taken off calendar as to issue 4 (punitive damages). Mr. Rocha's deposition testimony and declaration, defendant's responses to General Order 129 interrogatories and Mr. MacArthur's deposition testimony create a triable issue whether Mr. Rocha was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Ventfabrics flexible duct connectors) attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs represent that they have dismissed their claim for punitive damages. Order signed and will be filed when the Court completes the rulings on defendant's objections. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Erin Robinson, CSR 12199 of Aiken Welch (Tel: 510.451.1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT RHEEM MANUFACTURING CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances: Robert J. Bugatto of Vasquez Estrada & Conway LLP (415.453.0555) for defendant and moving party; Jennifer Alesio and Briana McCarthy of Brayton Purcell (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court takes the matter under submission. Counsel each provide proposed forms of orders and orders on objections for the Court's consideration. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Erin Robinson, CSR 12199 of Aiken Welch (Tel: 510.451.1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: INTERVENORS ON BEHALF OF THEIR SUSPENDED INSURED DEFENDANT HAMILTON SUPPLY COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances:
Alexandra M. Ozols of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (415.262.8535) for intervenors and moving parties; Jennifer Alesio and Briana McCarthy of Brayton Purcell (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Mr. Rocha's deposition testimony and declaration, Hamilton's 1975 Certificate of Ownership (Intervenors' Exhibit O) and plaintiffs' Exhibits C and D create a triable issue whether Mr. Rocha was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, they create a triable issue whether Hamilton Supply Company, Inc. is liable for Oakland Sheet Metal Supply Co., Inc. during the time period at issue here. Order signed, but it will be filed upon the Court's rulings on defendant's objections. Ms. Ozols is directed to submit a proposed order on defendant's objections. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Erin Robinson, CSR 12199 of Aiken Welch (Tel: 510.451.1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar May-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Eugene Rocha and Gail Rocha
Shelley K. Tinkoff of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / opposing party Union Carbide Corporation
Robert Bugatto of VASQUEZ ESTRADA & CONWAY LLP (415-453-0555), appearing for defendant / opposing party Rheem Manufacturing Company
(Refer to Sign In sheet for all other appearances.)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted per C.C.P. 36(d). Trial is September 23, 2013 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day for trial is September 27, 2013. Before any motions in limine are filed with the court the defendants are ordered to meet and confer on all motions in limine in order to avoid frivolous and duplicative hearings. If the parties do not meet and confer on the motions in limine the court will take appropriate actions. Last day for hearing summary motions is September 18, 2013. Summary motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant sections of the C.C.P. The time to respond to written discovery is shortened to 20 days. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than August 22, 2013. Responses to the demand are due no later than August 29, 2013. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on September 12, 2013. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 6, 2013. Expert discovery cut-off date is September 20, 2013. No later than June 11, 2013, plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than June 11, 2013, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. The parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. Further case management conference on July 31, 2013 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on September 23, 2013 at 1:30pm in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by September 19, 2013 at 2:00pm. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Miles Lendway of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13684 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
